title: Anacheilium pamplonense
taxonomy: Anacheilium pamplonense is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to Colombia and is a member of the genus Anacheilium. The classification of Anacheilium pamplonense places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Cymbidieae. This placement is consistent with the broader classification system for orchids. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification.
uses: Anacheilium pamplonense is a species of orchid, and its medicinal uses have not been extensively documented. properties: Anacheilium pamplonense, a species of orchid, is known to have potential therapeutic properties although not extensively studied. constituents: Anacheilium pamplonense contains flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. intro: Anacheilium pamplonense, also known as the Yellow Anacheilium, is a species of orchid native to Colombia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Anacheilium pamplonense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Anacheilium pamplonense is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat colds, fever, and skin conditions in folk medicine.
